rulings.Case Title: NATIONAL CREDIT ACCEPTANCE, INC A CALIFORNIAN CORP VS. MAHENDRA JHINGAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Order Granting An Earnings Withholding On The Spouse Of A Judgment Debtor And Req. For Judicial Ntc
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, October 11, 2012, at 9:00 am, line 1, ASSIGNEE SACOR FINANCIAL, INC. Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting An Earnings Withholding On The Spouse Of A Judgment Debtor And Request For Judicial Notice Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Denied without prejudice. Moving party failed to submit courtesy copies of the moving papers as required by Local Rule 2.6B. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: MIGUEL A. PALMA VS. PRUDENTIAL INSURANCE COMPANY OF AMERICA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, October 11, 2012, Line 21, DEFENDANT PRUDENTIAL INSURANCE COMPANY OF AMERICA's MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Granted as to the misrepresentation and intentional infliction of emotional distress causes of action and denied as to the bad faith cause of action and request for punitive damages. Defendant satisfied its burden of production and plaintiff failed to present evidence creating a triable issue as to the misrepresentation and intentional infliction claims. There is a triable issue whether defendant reasonably denied benefits where the nurse that investigated plaintiff's disability claim did not address plaintiff's specific job duties. (Chapkovich depo, 54:4-25; Laccetti depo., 58:5-59:4). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: BENITO CARRERA et al VS. TRACTEL, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Quash Service Of Summons; No-Cal Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, October 11, 2012, Line 6, CROSS DEFENDANT STAVELEY SERVICES CANADA, INC.'s motion To Quash Service Of Summons. Granted. Tractel has not satisfied its burden that Staveley Services Canada, Inc. has the requisite minimum contacts with California to subject it to either general or specific jurisdiction. Nor has Tractel shown that jurisdictional discovery is likely to produce evidence of such contacts. Arterra Owners Association did not file any opposition to the motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, October 11, 2012, at 9:00 am, line 2, PLAINTIFF RICHARD VANOSTEN Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set One And For Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. rulings.Case Title: KARLA SCHLAGS VS. CLOUD 9 LIMOSUINE, INC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, October 11, 2012, Line 10, DEFENDANT CLOUD 9 LIMOUSINE & TRANSPORTATION, INC.'s DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Sustained without leave to amend as to the cause of action for common carrier liability and overruled as to all other causes of action. Plaintiffs concede that common carrier liability does not apply to this case. At page 5 of the amended complaint plaintiffs allege that defendant provided alcohol and/or drugs to the intoxicated minors. There is a factual issue not amenable to resolution on demurrer whether the exception to immunity under Business and Professions Code 25602.1 applies to this case. Defendant owed a duty not to provide drugs to intoxicated minors. Plaintiffs are required to file their opposition in this case. Plaintiffs mistakenly filed the opposition in case number 521553. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. Plaintiffs are required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A., A NATIONAL BANKING VS. MARIA A ALFARO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Order Deeming Pltf'S First Set Of Requests For Admission Admitted Propounded On Deft Rene Alfaro; Request For Sanctions And Order Thereon; Declaration Of Lisa E Spiwak; P&A In Support Thereof (Ccp Sections 2033.280; 2023.010)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, October 11, 2012, at 9:00 am, line 4, PLAINTIFF WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A., A NATIONAL BANKING Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Order Deeming Pltf'S First Set Of Requests For Admission Admitted Propounded On Deft Rene Alfaro; Request For Sanctions And Order Thereon; Declaration Of Lisa E Spiwak; P&A In Support Thereof (Ccp Sections 2033.280; 2023.010) Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion to deem requests for admissions admitted is DENIED. Plaintiff has served responses prior to the hearing on this motion. Sanctions are awarded in the amount of $497.50. Plaintiff shall pay the sanctions to Wells Fargo Bank, N.A. within 30 days of entry of this order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/JPT

If the tentative ruling is not contested, the court will sign the proposed order submitted by the moving party without need for any appearances. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: GREATNONPROFITS VS. EXYGY, LLC
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Stay Arbitration; Memo Of P&A
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, October 11, 2012, Line 18, PLAINTIFF GREATNONPROFITS' Motion To Stay Arbitration. Denied. Exygy did not waive arbitration by filing and prosecuting case number 520971 against Mr. McManus. In that action, Exygy did not allege any agency relationship between Mr. McManus and GreatNonprofits. Exygy asserted a claim for intentional interference with contractual relations against Mr. McManus. Mr. McManus is not a party to the Terms & Agreement or a party to the arbitration. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)
